page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-2 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads per core. The instance is configured with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators, and comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is strong, ranking in the top 25% with a score of 2921.98. However, multi-core performance is weak, scoring 2926.09 in stress-ng and 2013.0 in Geekbench. Memory performance is a notable bottleneck; the Passmark memory score and small-block read-only memory bandwidth of 136.6 GB/sec both rank in the bottom 10%. Similarly, database operations and Redis SET operations fall into the bottom 10% performance tier.

Given the low memory allocation of 4.0 GB and poor memory bandwidth, this server is not suited for memory-intensive applications or heavy database workloads. However, its dedicated CPU allocation and average single-threaded performance make it a cost-efficient option for lightweight, compute-bound tasks that do not require large memory footprints. Appropriate workloads include basic single-threaded processing, lightweight web serving, and small-scale background workers.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-2 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs from a single physical AMD EPYC 7B13 core and 4.0 GB of RAM. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show a strong single-core CPU capability, ranking in the top 25% on stress-ng, but multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are weak, with memory read-only bandwidth and database operations scoring in the bottom 10%. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this instance a cost-efficient choice for lightweight, compute-bound tasks that do not demand high memory capacity. It is best suited for single-threaded processing, basic text processing, and lightweight web serving. The server also features cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA-NI flags, supporting secure operations despite its modest hardware footprint.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-2 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 4 GB of RAM, delivering strong single-core performance alongside highly constrained memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-2 features 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 4 GB RAM, optimized for lightweight, compute-bound workloads.
tagline: A dedicated dual-vCPU compute instance delivering strong single-core performance and efficient processing for memory-light, single-threaded workloads and lightweight web serving.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs
Configured with 4.0 GB of system memory and no local storage
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25%
Constrained memory bandwidth and database performance in the bottom 10%
Best suited for lightweight web serving and single-threaded processing
